title: Uzbekistan vs Colombia: World Cup 2026 Prediction | Squawka
original_text:
Uzbekistan vs Colombia kicks off on Wednesday, June 17 at 10:00 PM ET / 9:00 PM CT / 7:00 PM PT, with the White Wolves making their World Cup debut against Colombia in Group K at the Estadio Azteca in Mexico City.
Our Uzbekistan vs Colombia prediction starts with the occasion. Uzbekistan have reached a World Cup for the first time in their history, and they could hardly have drawn a tougher opening test than a Colombia side packed with European talent. The setting raises the stakes further, with the Azteca’s altitude waiting for both teams.
Mexico City sits at roughly 2,250 meters, and the thin air is a leveler. It saps the legs late and rewards patience, which can blunt a favorite’s tempo. Colombia carry the quality, but a tournament debut at altitude is rarely the straightforward night the odds suggest.
bet365 prices Colombia at -250 to win, with the draw at +350 and Uzbekistan out at +650. That line reflects the gulf in pedigree, though an opener at this altitude carries its own friction.
Market consensus: Kalshi traders price Uzbekistan at 10%, the draw at 20%, and Colombia at 72%. Full breakdown below.
Uzbekistan vs Colombia: Best Bets & Predictions
We side with Colombia, but the goal markets point toward a controlled, low-scoring win rather than a rout.

Odds correct at the time of writing. Please gamble responsibly.

Group K — 2026 FIFA World Cup
Group K pairs Colombia and Uzbekistan with Portugal and DR Congo. The table below reflects the draw rather than any played fixtures, with everyone level on zero before the opener. For the full bracket and group breakdown, see our World Cup 2026 hub.
TeamPosGPWDLGFGAGDPts
Portugal 1 0

Uzbekistan 3 0

Potential Match-Winners
Luis Díaz (Colombia): The Bayern Munich winger scored 15 goals in the Bundesliga this season as the champions ran away with the title, and he is Colombia’s most dangerous runner in behind.
Eldor Shomurodov (Uzbekistan): The İstanbul Başakşehir striker scored 22 goals in 33 Süper Lig matches this season, and he is the focal point Uzbekistan will build their attack around.
Abdukodir Khusanov (Uzbekistan): The Manchester City defender, a €40 million signing in early 2025, anchors the Uzbek back line and gives them a genuine top-level presence at the back.
Head-to-Head
There is no head-to-head history to lean on here. Uzbekistan and Colombia have never met in a competitive senior international, so Wednesday’s opener is a genuine first meeting between the two nations.
Form Coming Into the Tournament
Uzbekistan reach the World Cup for the first time after coming through Asian qualifying, a landmark for a program that has knocked on the door for years. The side mixes domestic regulars with players earning minutes in Europe, and the challenge now is translating qualifying belief to the biggest stage of all, away from home and at altitude.
Colombia arrive with momentum and a deep pool of talent spread across Europe’s top leagues. They navigated CONMEBOL qualifying, one of the toughest routes in world soccer, with a settled core built around Díaz and a creative midfield. The opener at the Azteca is a chance to set the tone in Group K and ease the pressure before sterner tests arrive.
This has the makings of a controlled night for Colombia. They should dominate the ball, manage the tempo at altitude, and find a way through a disciplined Uzbek block, even if the margin stays narrow. Our lean is Colombia and a low-scoring game, so back Colombia to win at -250 with bet365. For the wider market, our World Cup 2026 outright odds page tracks the title picture, and you can read our full bet365 review before you bet. Kick-off is 10:00 PM ET at Estadio Azteca, Mexico City.
Odds correct at the time of writing. Please gamble responsibly.
